greetings.example: Hey Ahmed! How's it going? — Pretty good, thanks!
What's up, man? — Not much, just studying.
I'm Sara. Nice to meet you! — Nice to meet you too!
Hey, how are you doing? — I'm doing great, thanks!
Good to see you! It's been a while.

opinions.example: I think English is easier than people say.
In my opinion, practice is more important than grammar.
If you ask me, you should start with listening.
I feel like my accent is getting better.
The way I see it, consistency beats intensity.

reactions.example: Oh, that makes sense! I didn't think of it that way.
You learned English in 6 months? No way!
Really? I had no idea that's how you say it.
You passed the exam? That's awesome!
You got the job? Are you serious? Congratulations!

clarifying.example: Sorry, what do you mean by that?
Could you say that again? I didn't catch it.
In other words, you want me to start over?

clarifying.phrase: Let me make sure I understand...
clarifying.example: Let me make sure I understand — you want the blue one?

clarifying.when: Polite way to ask for repetition
clarifying.example: Sorry, I didn't catch that. Could you speak a bit slower?

transitions.example: By the way, have you seen the new movie?

transitions.when: Connecting to what was just said
transitions.example: Speaking of which, I need to buy groceries too.

transitions.when: Getting back to the main point
transitions.example: Anyway, what I was trying to say is...
So basically, you need to practice every day.
The thing is, I don't have enough time.
